rationale: The two notes indicate strong methodological overlap: both papers address multiattribute preference measurement for complex products using paired-comparison style elicitation and both benchmark against ACA, while the citing paper explicitly discusses limitations of self-explicated methods. That makes the cited ASE paper a plausible method/comparison source rather than a generic background mention. Although the raw evidence is only one DOI-matched reference entry (no in-text snippet), this is not safely droppable as mention-only.
